seo_title: India's Gig Economy Trains Robots for AI and Robotics
meta_description: Human Archive taps into India's services startups to collect data for physical AI training, enabling robots to learn from real-world experiences.
body_html:
Direct answer: Human Archive, a startup founded by UC Berkeley and Stanford researchers, is paying gig workers in India to wear camera-equipped caps and sensor devices to collect real-world physical training data for AI and robotics labs.

source.title: This startup is betting India’s gig economy can train the world’s robots
source.summary: A startup called Human Archive is using India's gig economy to train robots. The company pays gig workers to wear special devices with cameras and sensors, which collect real-world data for artificial intelligence and robotics research. This data will help improve the performance of robots in various environments.
